lede: A look at July 14 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Woody Guthrie and Ingmar Bergman, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2016 Nice truck attack
events.desc: A man ploughs a truck into a Bastille Day celebration in Nice, France, killing 86 people and injuring another 434 before being shot by police.

events.title: Korean War
events.desc: Korean War: beginning of the Battle of Taejon.

events.title: Battle of Delville Wood
events.desc: Battle of Delville Wood begins as an action within the Battle of the Somme, lasting until 3 September 1916.

events.title: St Mark's Campanile
events.desc: The Campanile in St Mark's Square, Venice collapses, also demolishing the loggetta.

events.title: Billy the Kid
events.desc: American outlaw Billy the Kid is shot and killed by Sheriff Pat Garrett in the Maxwell House at Fort Sumner, New Mexico.

events.title: Chicago Fire of 1874
events.desc: The Chicago Fire of 1874 burns down 47 acres of the city, destroying 812 buildings, killing 20, and resulting in the fire insurance industry demanding municipal reforms from Chicago's city council.

events.title: First ascent of the Matterhorn
events.desc: The first ascent of the Matterhorn is completed by Edward Whymper and his party, four of whom die on the descent.

events.title: Alien and Sedition Acts
events.desc: The Sedition Act of 1798 becomes law in the United States making it a federal crime to write, publish, or utter false or malicious statements about the United States government.

events.title: Priestley Riots
events.desc: Beginning of Priestley Riots (to 17 July) in Birmingham targeting Joseph Priestley as a supporter of the French Revolution.

events.title: Louis VIII of France
events.desc: Louis VIII becomes King of France upon the death of his father, Philip II.

births.name: Gerald Ford
births.line: American commander, lawyer, and politician, 38th President of the United States

births.name: Ante Pavelić
births.line: Croatian fascist dictator during World War II

births.name: Navin Ramgoolam
births.line: Mauritius physician and politician, 3rd Prime Minister of Mauritius

deaths.text: Jacinto Benavente, Spanish author and playwright, Nobel Prize laureate
Billy the Kid, American gunfighter and outlaw (born 1859 or 1860)
Germaine de Staël, French philosopher and author
Philip II of France, king of France

observances.text: Christian feast day: Camillus of Lellis (Roman Catholic Church, except in the United States)
Christian feast day: John Keble (Church of England)
Christian feast day: Samson Occom (Episcopal Church (United States)
